steps.title: Focus on the scroll contrast
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two scrolls in Landscape and Couplet of Chinese Verse. The notes give the relevant contrast: Scroll 1 features a painting, and Scroll 2 features a poem from Li Bai.

steps.title: Reject similarities and errors
steps.content: Choice B emphasizes what the scrolls have in common by saying they each depict nature, so it does not emphasize a difference. Choice C incorrectly says Li Bai created the artwork. Choice D invents a portrait of Li Bai, which is not in the notes.

steps.title: Keep to the notes
steps.content: The notes do not ask for background about Taiga, Li Bai, or nature as a shared subject. They ask for the difference between the two parts of the artwork, so the answer has to contrast the scrolls directly.

steps.title: State the actual difference
steps.content: The sentence that contrasts one scroll with a painting and the other scroll with a poem uses only information from the notes and directly addresses the student’s goal of showing how the scrolls differ. The answer is A.
